title: uragoga subrubra
side_effects: Uragoga subrubra can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal problems, allergic reactions, and interactions with other medications. Using this plant without proper knowledge and guidance can lead to stomach cramps, diarrhea, and nausea, which can be exacerbated in individuals with pre-existing digestive conditions. In severe cases, improper use of uragoga subrubra can also cause anaphylaxis, a life-threatening allergic reaction that requires immediate medical attention. Furthermore, combining uragoga subrubra with other medications can lead to adverse interactions, such as increased blood pressure or altered blood sugar levels.
